body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The user is asking whether `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` (v12.3.0) supports a server-side / URL-to-URL copy from a SharePoint Online or Microsoft Graph download URL directly into an Azure Blob container — without needing to first download the file locally. This was possible with the legacy `Microsoft.Azure.Storage.DataMovement` library.

---

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

### Issue Type
This is a **feature/capability question** — the user wants to know whether the new DataMovement library supports an equivalent of the old library's URL-to-URL (stream-from-URI) copy operation for external (non-Azure) HTTP sources like SharePoint/Graph.

### Current Library Behavior
The new `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` SDK is built on a `StorageResource` abstraction model. The core library has internal primitives for `UriToStreamJobPart` and `StreamToUriJobPart`, but the built-in `StorageResourceProvider` implementations only support **Azure-to-Azure** transfers (Blob ↔ Blob, Blob ↔ File Share, Blob ↔ Local File, etc.).

There does **not** appear to be a built-in `HttpStorageResourceProvider` or equivalent that allows an arbitrary external HTTP/HTTPS URL (like a SharePoint/Graph download link) to be used as a source without local download/upload roundtrip.

### Workarounds to Mention
1. **Custom `StorageResourceItem`**: The DataMovement library is extensible. A developer could implement a custom `StorageResourceItem` backed by an `HttpClient` reading from the Graph/SharePoint download URL and use it as the source in a transfer.
2. **`BlobClient.SyncCopyFromUriAsync`**: Azure Blob Storage's REST API supports server-side copy from a public or SAS-authenticated URL via [Put Block from URL](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/put-block-from-url) and [Copy Blob from URL](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/copy-blob-from-url). If the SharePoint download link is accessible without additional auth headers, this could work directly. However, SharePoint/Graph URLs typically require an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, which Azure Blob's server-side copy REST API does **not** support for external OAuth-protected URLs — making this approach unlikely to work without additional engineering.
3. **Stream with `BlobClient.UploadAsync` using a `Stream`**: Download the file as a `Stream` using `GraphServiceClient` and upload directly to `BlobClient.UploadAsync(stream)`, which avoids writing to disk but still goes through the client machine's network. This is the most practical near-term solution.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.Storage.DataMovement README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement/README.md)
- [Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/README.md)
- [DataMovement Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs/samples/)
- [StorageResourceItem extensibility](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/storage/Azure.Storage.DataMovement/src/StorageResourceItem.cs)
- [Copy Blob from URL REST API docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/copy-blob-from-url)
- [Put Block from URL REST API docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/storageservices/put-block-from-url)
- [Microsoft Graph download large files](https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/driveitem-get-content)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🧑‍💻 Suggested Checklist for Team</summary>

- [ ] **Clarify** whether an external HTTP URL source is officially supported or planned for `Azure.Storage.DataMovement`
- [ ] **Document** whether implementing a custom `StorageResourceItem` wrapping an `HttpClient` stream is the recommended extensibility path for this scenario
- [ ] **Evaluate** whether a first-class `HttpStorageResourceProvider` (for external URLs) would be a worthwhile addition to the DataMovement library, given the user demand for copying from external services like SharePoint/Graph
- [ ] **Respond** to the user with the recommended workaround (stream-based upload via Graph SDK + `BlobClient.UploadAsync`, or custom `StorageResourceItem`) and whether a server-side-only path is feasible

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔍 Similar Issues / Notes</summary>

No duplicate open issues were found for this exact feature request. The user references legacy support in `Microsoft.Azure.Storage.DataMovement`, which did allow specifying a source URL for copying. The new SDK generation redesigned this model — confirming or clarifying feature parity gaps here would be valuable.

</details>
